description.en: An overview of how banks function and their role in the economy.
description.ru: Обзор того, как работают банки и их роль в экономике.
description.kz: Банктердің қалай жұмыс істеуі және олардың экономикадағы рөлі туралы шолу.

description.en: Banks connect people who have money today with people who need money today, making the economy more productive.

body.en: Imagine thousands of people saving money and thousands of others needing loans. Without a system connecting them, most savings would sit idle while useful projects went unfunded. Banks exist to bridge that gap.

tip.en: A bank's core job is moving money from where it is unused to where it can be used productively.

body.en: Many depositors place money into accounts for safety and convenience. Banks combine these deposits and lend part of the pool to households and businesses that need financing.

example.en: One person's 500,000 ₸ deposit may help fund part of a business loan worth millions of tenge.

body.en: Banks do more than hold cash. They provide payment systems, transfers, cards, savings accounts, loans, and secure record-keeping. These services make modern economic activity possible.

tip.en: Most people interact with banks daily even when they never visit a branch.

title.en: How money flows through a bank

blocks.text: A simplified example shows how banks connect savers and borrowers.
The saver keeps access to banking services while the borrower gains capital to invest.

body.en: Classify each situation according to whether it mainly involves storing money or providing financing.

en.instruction: Sort each item into the category that best describes the bank's primary role.
en.explanation: Banks perform both functions. They help people safely store and move money, while also directing savings toward borrowers who need capital.

body.en: Banks sit between savers and borrowers. By pooling deposits, managing payments, and extending loans, they make money more useful across the economy.

tip.en: When evaluating a bank later, remember that every product ultimately supports either storing money, moving money, or lending money.

question_text.en: A city has many households saving money but very few ways for businesses to borrow. What problem are banks primarily solving?

answer_text.en: Connecting idle savings with people and businesses that need financing
Creating wealth without risk
Guaranteeing that all businesses succeed
Eliminating the need for savings

question_text.en: Suppose everyone kept cash at home instead of using banks. Which banking function would become much harder?

answer_text.en: Matching large numbers of savers with borrowers
Printing physical banknotes
Setting government tax rates
Producing consumer goods

question_text.en: A bank customer receives salary payments, pays bills, and later takes a car loan. What does this illustrate?

answer_text.en: Banks combine payment services and financing services
Banks only exist to issue loans
Banks mainly store physical cash in vaults
Banks are unnecessary for everyday payments

description.en: Different bank accounts exist for different jobs. Choosing the wrong account can cost money or reduce flexibility.

body.en: People often think a bank account is just a place to keep money. In reality, different accounts are designed for different goals: spending, saving, earning interest, or managing a business.

tip.en: Choosing an account is really choosing which financial job you need it to perform.

body.en: A current account (sometimes called a checking account) is built for frequent transactions. Salaries arrive here, bills are paid from here, and cards are usually linked to it.

example.en: A person earning 450,000 ₸ monthly may receive income into a current account and use it for everyday spending.

body.en: Savings accounts and term deposits focus on preserving money and earning interest. They usually offer better returns than transaction accounts, but may limit access or withdrawals.

tip.en: Money needed tomorrow belongs in a transaction account; money not needed soon may belong in savings.

blocks.text: Different goals require different account types.
The best account depends on how and when the money will be used.

body.en: Decide which account type best fits each situation.

en.instruction: Select the most suitable account category for each scenario.

scenarios.text: You need money available for daily purchases, transfers, and monthly bills.
scenarios.explanation: Frequent transactions require easy access and payment functionality.

scenarios.text: You are building an emergency fund that may not be touched for many months.
scenarios.explanation: The goal is preservation and interest, not daily spending.

scenarios.text: Your employer sends your salary every month and you use a debit card regularly.
scenarios.explanation: This is the classic purpose of a transaction account.

body.en: Each account type solves a different problem. Instead of asking which account is best, ask what job the money needs to do and choose the account that fits that job.

tip.en: Many people benefit from using more than one account: one for spending and one for savings.

question_text.en: Aigerim plans to use money for rent, groceries, and transfers throughout the month. Which account is most appropriate?

answer_text.en: Current account
Long-term deposit account
Investment fund
Insurance policy

question_text.en: A family has 1,200,000 ₸ reserved for emergencies and expects to leave it untouched for a long period. What is the strongest reason to place it in a savings account rather than a transaction account?

answer_text.en: It may earn interest while remaining relatively accessible
It guarantees higher investment returns than stocks
It removes all risk from inflation
It prevents withdrawals under any circumstances

question_text.en: A person keeps all money in a term deposit but frequently needs cash for daily expenses. What is the main problem?

answer_text.en: The account's purpose does not match the money's intended use
Term deposits cannot hold money safely
Banks prohibit spending deposited money
Interest is never paid on deposits

title.en: How Banks Make Money (and How They Charge You)

description.en: Banks earn profit by charging for services and using deposits to generate interest income through lending.

body.en: Banks feel like infrastructure, but they are profit-driven companies. They earn money by charging fees and by earning interest on loans funded by your deposits.

tip.en: If a service is free in banking, you are usually paying in another indirect way.

title.en: Interest spread: the core profit engine

body.en: Banks pay depositors a low interest rate and charge borrowers a higher rate. The difference between these two is called the interest spread, and it is a major source of profit.

example.en: A bank pays 5% annual interest on deposits but lends money at 18%. The 13% difference helps cover costs and generate profit.

body.en: Banks also charge direct fees for services such as account maintenance, card issuance, transfers, ATM withdrawals, and foreign currency operations.

tip.en: Small recurring fees matter more than they seem because they accumulate over years.

title.en: Where your money goes in practice

blocks.text: A single bank account can generate income for the bank in multiple ways.
Even if you do not see fees directly, the system still generates revenue from your activity.

body.en: Match each situation to how the bank is earning money.

en.instruction: Choose whether the bank earns mainly from interest spread or fees.

scenarios.text: A customer takes a car loan at 16% annual interest.
scenarios.explanation: The bank earns profit from lending money at a higher rate than it pays on deposits.

scenarios.text: A bank charges 300 ₸ per international transfer.
scenarios.explanation: This is a direct service charge unrelated to lending.

scenarios.text: A credit card transaction generates a small percentage paid by the merchant.
scenarios.explanation: Merchant transaction fees are part of banking fee income.

title.en: Bank profit comes from information and access

body.en: Banks profit because they control access to capital and manage risk better than individuals can alone. Their income comes mainly from lending spreads and service fees.

tip.en: Understanding bank revenue helps you notice where hidden costs appear in everyday banking.

question_text.en: A bank pays 4% interest on deposits and charges 15% on loans. What is the main source of profit here?

answer_text.en: The interest rate difference between loans and deposits
Printing new money
Government subsidies only
Charging taxes on income

question_text.en: Which of the following is an example of a bank fee rather than interest income?

answer_text.en: Monthly account maintenance charge
Interest earned on a mortgage loan
Interest paid on savings accounts
Government bond yield

question_text.en: Why can small banking fees still matter a lot over time?

answer_text.en: They accumulate repeatedly over many transactions and years
They are always refunded automatically
They only apply to large corporations
They are unrelated to bank profit

description.en: How deposit insurance protects your money in case a bank fails, and what limits and conditions actually apply.

title.en: What happens if a bank collapses?

body.en: Banks are generally stable, but they can fail. Deposit insurance exists to prevent people from losing their savings if a bank becomes insolvent.

tip.en: The goal is not to make banks risk-free, but to protect ordinary depositors from losing everything.

body.en: In Kazakhstan, deposit insurance is provided by the Kazakhstan Deposit Insurance Fund (KDIF). It guarantees repayment of eligible deposits up to a legal limit if a licensed bank fails.

example.en: If a bank fails and you had 3,000,000 ₸ in insured deposits within the limit, you receive compensation from the fund, not the bank.

title.en: What is covered and what is not

body.en: Typically, standard deposits in local currency and foreign currency accounts are covered up to a maximum limit per depositor per bank. Investment products are usually not covered.

tip.en: Protection applies per bank, not per account type — multiple accounts in one bank are aggregated.

blocks.text: Deposit insurance has a cap, which means not all money may be protected if balances are very large.
Risk is not about losing everything instantly — it is about exceeding coverage limits or holding uninsured products.

body.en: Identify whether the money is covered by deposit insurance in typical cases.

en.instruction: Select whether the funds are likely insured under KDIF rules.

scenarios.text: A fixed-term deposit in a licensed Kazakhstani bank within insurance limits.
scenarios.explanation: Standard deposits in insured banks are protected up to the legal cap.

scenarios.text: Investment in a foreign stock ETF through a broker.
scenarios.explanation: Investment products are not deposit accounts and are not insured.

scenarios.text: Savings held in a current account at a licensed bank.
scenarios.explanation: Transaction accounts are also included in deposit insurance coverage.

title.en: Insurance reduces bank risk for individuals

body.en: Deposit insurance ensures that most ordinary savers are protected even if a bank fails, up to a defined limit. It does not eliminate risk completely, but it makes the system stable and predictable.

tip.en: A good habit is knowing your insurance limit so you never accidentally exceed it in one bank.

question_text.en: What is the main purpose of deposit insurance?

answer_text.en: To protect depositors if a bank becomes insolvent
To increase bank profits
To eliminate all financial risk in the economy
To replace the banking system entirely

question_text.en: A person holds deposits across multiple accounts in the same bank exceeding the insurance limit. What happens?

answer_text.en: Only the insured amount is protected, the rest is at risk
Each account is fully insured separately with no limit
The government guarantees unlimited compensation
Banks automatically double the coverage

question_text.en: Which of the following is typically NOT covered by deposit insurance?

answer_text.en: Bank savings account deposit
Fixed-term deposit in a licensed bank
ETF investment through a broker
Current account balance

description.en: A practical framework for choosing a bank based on costs, reliability, and usability instead of marketing.

body.en: People often choose banks based on brand familiarity or bonuses. In reality, the right bank is the one that minimizes your costs and friction for how you actually use money.

tip.en: A bank is a long-term service relationship, not a one-time choice.

title.en: Cost structure matters more than perks

body.en: Banks differ in fees for transfers, cards, withdrawals, and currency exchange. Small differences in fees can compound into large losses over years of usage.

example.en: A 200 ₸ monthly maintenance fee equals 2,400 ₸ per year, which becomes 24,000 ₸ over 10 years without any added value.

body.en: A good bank is stable, regulated, and has strong digital access. In Kazakhstan, users also care about app quality, instant payments, and ATM network coverage.

tip.en: A cheap bank that is hard to use is more expensive in lost time and friction.

blocks.text: Two banks can look similar but differ significantly in total cost of use.
Over time, Bank A is cheaper and more convenient even if both look similar on the surface.

body.en: Select which bank is more rational based on real usage patterns.

en.instruction: Pick the better option for each situation.

scenarios.text: You make frequent transfers and want to minimize ongoing fees.
scenarios.explanation: Bank A has lower or zero transfer fees, reducing long-term cost.

scenarios.text: You rarely use transfers but care about lowest upfront monthly cost.
scenarios.explanation: Even in low usage, Bank A avoids recurring fees.

scenarios.text: You value a smooth mobile app experience for daily banking.
scenarios.explanation: Better usability reduces friction and hidden time costs.

title.en: Choose based on usage, not marketing

body.en: The best bank is the one aligned with your actual financial behavior. Evaluate fees, usability, and reliability together rather than focusing on promotions or brand popularity.

tip.en: Re-evaluate your bank when your financial habits change.

title.en: How to Choose a Bank — Check

question_text.en: What is the most important factor when choosing a bank?

answer_text.en: How well it matches your actual usage and costs
Which bank has the most advertisements
Which bank offers the largest sign-up bonus
Which bank is the oldest in the country

question_text.en: A bank charges small monthly fees and high transfer fees. Why can this become expensive over time?

answer_text.en: Because repeated small costs accumulate across many transactions
Because banks refund all fees annually
Because fees only apply once per lifetime
Because transfer fees are always fixed by government law

question_text.en: Why is bank usability (like mobile app quality) financially important?

answer_text.en: It reduces friction and prevents costly mistakes or delays
It increases interest rates automatically
It replaces the need for money management
It guarantees zero fees
